name: Emoji stress set
source: Curated for Prelight v0.1.x F6. Grapheme cluster stress cases covering every shape the Unicode emoji spec prescribes: base, ZWJ sequences up to 7 joiners, skin-tone modifiers (all 5 Fitzpatrick levels), regional-indicator flags, keycaps, tag sequences (subdivision flags), variation-selector forms, and recently-added characters through Emoji 15.1 (2023). Every entry is a single grapheme unless the key ends in _run, _prefix, or _suffix.
notes: Browsers render these as single grapheme clusters when the installed font supports the sequence. When the font does not, fallback behaviour varies: a family ZWJ may render as 4 separate heads on one engine and a single glyph on another. Prelight's job is to agree with whatever the user's chosen font actually renders, not to re-shape grapheme clusters itself. Pretext already segments on grapheme-cluster boundaries; Prelight inherits that and adds no post-correction for emoji. Release-gate floor: 93% per engine (DECISIONS #008).
